title: Lime Leaf Braise
description: An earthy and juicy diabetic-friendly Italian dish featuring lime leaves that's perfect for a sick-day meal.

ingredients.name: Lime Leaves
ingredients.notes: Use fresh or dried, depending on availability

ingredients.name: Chicken Breast
ingredients.notes: Skin-on for extra flavor and nutrition

ingredients.name: Thyme
ingredients.notes: Fresh or dried, optional but enhances flavor

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: For sautéing and browning the chicken

ingredients.name: Vegetable Broth
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's low-sodium or use a diabetic-friendly alternative

instructions: Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Sear chicken on both sides until golden brown and slightly crispy, about 5 minutes per side.
Add onion and garlic; sauté for another minute or two until fragrant.
Stir in thyme if using, then pour in vegetable broth. Bring to a simmer, scraping the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon to deglaze.
Place chicken on top of vegetables, cover tightly with tin foil and transfer to oven. Braise for 25-30 minutes or until chicken is cooked through (internal temperature reaches at least 165°F).
Remove from heat and let rest for a few minutes before serving. Garnish with sour cream if desired.
